Individual Assignment - Global Sanitation & Health: A UNICEF Data Story

Produced for Data Analytics and Story Telling (DCU)

Author

Anmol Brijesh Shami - A00054400

Published

April 7, 2026

Code 
!pip install polars plotnine geopandas==0.14.4 pycountry-convert pyarrow --quiet

Introduction

Access to safe sanitation remains one of the most critical yet under-addressed global challenges. Millions of people lack proper waste treatment, exposing communities to preventable diseases. This report uses UNICEF data to explore sanitation coverage, maternal health indicators, and life expectancy patterns across the globe — revealing the deep connections between infrastructure, health outcomes, and economic development.

Code 
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
import geopandas as gpd
from plotnine import *
import pycountry_convert as pc
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# ── Custom plotnine theme ──
theme_unicef = (
    theme_minimal()
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(size=15, weight="bold", color="#1a3a5c",
                                margin={"b": 6}),
        plot_subtitle=element_text(size=10, color="#666666", style="italic",
                                    margin={"b": 12}),
        axis_title_x=element_text(size=10, color="#444444", margin={"t": 10}),
        axis_title_y=element_text(size=10, color="#444444", margin={"r": 10}),
        axis_text=element_text(size=9, color="#555555"),
        legend_title=element_text(size=10, weight="bold", color="#333333"),
        legend_text=element_text(size=9, color="#444444"),
        legend_background=element_rect(fill="white", alpha=0.8),
        legend_key=element_rect(fill="white", color="white"),
        panel_grid_major=element_line(color="#eeeeee", size=0.4),
        panel_grid_minor=element_blank(),
        plot_background=element_rect(fill="white", color="white"),
        panel_background=element_rect(fill="white"),
        strip_text=element_text(size=10, weight="bold", color="#1a3a5c"),
        plot_margin=0.035,
    )
)

# ── Colour palettes ──
continent_pal = {
    "Africa": "#e63946",
    "Asia": "#457b9d",
    "Europe": "#2a9d8f",
    "North America": "#e9c46a",
    "Oceania": "#f4a261",
    "South America": "#264653",
}
continent_colors = list(continent_pal.values())

map_theme = (
    theme_unicef
    + theme(
        figure_size=(13, 6.5),
        axis_text=element_blank(),
        axis_ticks=element_blank(),
        axis_title=element_blank(),
        panel_grid_major=element_blank(),
    )
)

# ── Load data from Google Drive ──
indicator_1 = pl.read_csv("/content/drive/MyDrive/unicef_indicator_1.csv")
indicator_2 = pl.read_csv("/content/drive/MyDrive/unicef_indicator_2.csv")
metadata = pl.read_csv("/content/drive/MyDrive/unicef_metadata.csv")

# ── Helper: alpha_3 → continent ──
def get_continent(alpha3):
    try:
        alpha2 = pc.country_alpha3_to_country_alpha2(alpha3)
        continent_code = pc.country_alpha2_to_continent_code(alpha2)
        return pc.convert_continent_code_to_continent_name(continent_code)
    except Exception:
        return None

# ── Load world geometries ──
world = gpd.read_file(gpd.datasets.get_path("naturalearth_lowres"))
world = world.rename(columns={"iso_a3": "alpha_3_code"})

Exploring Sanitation Rates Across the World

The map below shows the proportion of the population using on-site sanitation facilities with human waste treated off-site by country, averaged over the most recent five years available. Higher values indicate stronger sanitation infrastructure.

Code 
latest = (
    indicator_1
    .filter(pl.col("time_period") >= 2019)
    .group_by("alpha_3_code", "country")
    .agg(pl.col("obs_value").mean().alias("avg_sanitation"))
)

merged = world.merge(latest.to_pandas(), on="alpha_3_code", how="left")

(
    ggplot(merged)
    + geom_map(aes(fill="avg_sanitation"), color="#ffffff", size=0.2)
    + scale_fill_gradient2(
        low="#f7fcf5", mid="#74c476", high="#00441b",
        midpoint=15, na_value="#f0f0f0",
        name="% Treated\nOff-Site"
    )
    + labs(title="Sanitation Coverage by Country",
           subtitle="Averaged over 2019–2024  ·  Darker shading = higher coverage")
    + map_theme
)

Top and Bottom Countries by Sanitation Coverage

Which nations lead in sanitation — and which are falling behind? This chart compares the top 15 against the bottom 15 countries.

Code 
latest_year = (
    indicator_1
    .group_by("country", "alpha_3_code")
    .agg(pl.col("time_period").max().alias("latest_year"))
)

latest_vals = (
    indicator_1
    .join(latest_year, on=["country", "alpha_3_code"])
    .filter(pl.col("time_period") == pl.col("latest_year"))
    .select("country", "obs_value")
    .sort("obs_value", descending=True)
)

top15 = latest_vals.head(15).with_columns(pl.lit("Top 15").alias("group"))
bot15 = latest_vals.tail(15).with_columns(pl.lit("Bottom 15").alias("group"))
bar_df = pl.concat([top15, bot15]).to_pandas()

(
    ggplot(bar_df, aes(x="reorder(country, obs_value)", y="obs_value", fill="group"))
    + geom_col(width=0.75, alpha=0.9)
    + geom_text(
        aes(label="round(obs_value, 1)"),
        ha="left", size=7, nudge_y=0.3, color="#333333"
    )
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_fill_manual(values={"Top 15": "#2a9d8f", "Bottom 15": "#e63946"})
    + labs(x="", y="Sanitation Coverage (%)", fill="",
           title="Countries with Highest & Lowest Sanitation Coverage",
           subtitle="Based on each country's most recent data year")
    + theme_unicef
    + theme(figure_size=(10, 9))
)

Scatterplot: GDP per Capita vs. Sanitation Coverage

Does national wealth translate into better sanitation? The plot below examines this relationship using 2020 data, with each bubble sized by population.

Code 
meta_2020 = (
    metadata
    .filter(pl.col("year") == 2020)
    .select("alpha_3_code", "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)",
            "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)", "Population, total")
    .rename({"GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)": "gdp_pc",
             "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)": "life_exp",
             "Population, total": "population"})
)

ind1_2020 = (
    indicator_1
    .filter(pl.col("time_period") == 2020)
    .select("alpha_3_code", "obs_value")
    .rename({"obs_value": "sanitation"})
)

scatter_df = (
    ind1_2020
    .join(meta_2020, on="alpha_3_code")
    .drop_nulls(["gdp_pc", "sanitation"])
)

scatter_pd = scatter_df.to_pandas()
scatter_pd["alpha_3_code"] = scatter_pd["alpha_3_code"].astype(str)
scatter_pd["continent"] = scatter_pd["alpha_3_code"].apply(get_continent)
scatter_pd = scatter_pd.dropna(subset=["continent"])

(
    ggplot(scatter_pd, aes(x="gdp_pc", y="sanitation",
                           color="continent", size="population"))
    + geom_point(alpha=0.6, stroke=0.3)
    + geom_smooth(aes(group=1), method="lm", se=True,
                  color="#1a3a5c", linetype="dashed", size=0.7, alpha=0.12)
    + scale_x_log10(labels=lambda x: [f"${v/1000:.0f}k" for v in x])
    + scale_size_continuous(range=(2, 14))
    + scale_color_manual(values=continent_pal)
    + guides(size=None)
    + labs(x="GDP per Capita (log scale, 2015 US$)",
           y="Sanitation Coverage (%)",
           color="Continent",
           title="Wealthier Nations Tend to Have Better Sanitation",
           subtitle="Bubble size = population  ·  Dashed line = linear trend")
    + theme_unicef
    + theme(figure_size=(11, 6.5))
)

Insights into Global Life Expectancy Trends

Tracking Life Expectancy Across Continents

Life expectancy has increased globally since 1960, but the pace of progress varies dramatically by region.

Code 
life_ts = (
    metadata
    .select("alpha_3_code", "year", "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)")
    .rename({"Life expectancy at birth, total (years)": "life_exp"})
    .drop_nulls()
    .filter(pl.col("year") >= 1960)
)

life_pd = life_ts.to_pandas()
life_pd["continent"] = life_pd["alpha_3_code"].apply(get_continent)
life_pd = life_pd.dropna(subset=["continent"])

continent_avg = (
    life_pd
    .groupby(["continent", "year"])["life_exp"]
    .mean()
    .reset_index()
)

# Label endpoints
endpoints = continent_avg.loc[
    continent_avg.groupby("continent")["year"].idxmax()
]

(
    ggplot(continent_avg, aes(x="year", y="life_exp", color="continent"))
    + geom_line(size=1.2, alpha=0.85)
    + geom_point(data=endpoints, size=2.5)
    + geom_text(
        data=endpoints,
        mapping=aes(label="continent"),
        ha="left", nudge_x=1, size=7.5, show_legend=False
    )
    + scale_color_manual(values=continent_pal)
    + scale_x_continuous(breaks=range(1960, 2030, 10))
    + labs(x="", y="Life Expectancy (years)", color="Continent",
           title="Life Expectancy Has Risen Globally — But Gaps Persist",
           subtitle="Continental averages, 1960–2022")
    + theme_unicef
    + theme(figure_size=(11, 5.5), legend_position="none")
)

Mapping Global Life Expectancy

Code 
life_2020 = (
    metadata
    .filter(pl.col("year") == 2020)
    .select("alpha_3_code", "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)")
    .rename({"Life expectancy at birth, total (years)": "life_exp"})
    .drop_nulls()
)

merged_life = world.merge(life_2020.to_pandas(), on="alpha_3_code", how="left")

(
    ggplot(merged_life)
    + geom_map(aes(fill="life_exp"), color="#ffffff", size=0.2)
    + scale_fill_gradient2(
        low="#67001f", mid="#f4a582", high="#053061",
        midpoint=72, na_value="#f0f0f0",
        name="Years"
    )
    + labs(title="Life Expectancy by Country (2020)",
           subtitle="Diverging palette centred at global median  ·  Blue = above, Red = below")
    + map_theme
)

Maternal Health: HIV Testing at Antenatal Clinics

The second UNICEF indicator tracks the number of pregnant women tested for HIV at antenatal clinics. The bar chart below highlights the countries with the highest reported numbers.

Code 
hiv_latest = (
    indicator_2
    .group_by("country")
    .agg([
        pl.col("obs_value").last().alias("tested"),
        pl.col("time_period").max().alias("year")
    ])
    .sort("tested", descending=True)
    .head(20)
)

hiv_pd = hiv_latest.to_pandas()

(
    ggplot(hiv_pd, aes(x="reorder(country, tested)", y="tested"))
    + geom_col(fill="#6a51a3", width=0.75, alpha=0.9)
    + geom_text(
        aes(label=[f"{v/1e6:.1f}M" if v >= 1e6 else f"{v/1e3:.0f}K"
                    for v in hiv_pd.sort_values("tested")["tested"]]),
        ha="left", size=7, nudge_y=5000, color="#333333"
    )
    + coord_flip()
    + scale_y_continuous(
        labels=lambda x: [f"{v/1e6:.1f}M" if v >= 1e6 else f"{v/1e3:.0f}K" for v in x]
    )
    + labs(x="", y="Number of Pregnant Women Tested",
           title="HIV Testing at Antenatal Clinics — Leading Countries",
           subtitle="Most recent year available per country")
    + theme_unicef
    + theme(figure_size=(10, 8))
)

Conclusion

The data paints a clear picture: sanitation, health infrastructure, and economic development are deeply intertwined. Countries with higher GDP per capita tend to provide better sanitation coverage, and regions with stronger health systems — reflected in higher life expectancy and broader maternal HIV testing — are better positioned to protect their most vulnerable populations. While global life expectancy has improved markedly since 1960, significant disparities remain between continents, underscoring the continued need for targeted investment in public health and sanitation infrastructure.
